vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-
vulnerability.group: Verify Permissions on Files with Local Account Information and Credentials

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the permissions of a backup gshadow file, which does not affect active authentication mechanisms or system boot. File permission adjustments are standard STIG hardening actions and pose minimal risk of lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Typographical error could set incorrect permissions; Incorrect path could modify a non‑backup file
